facilities.title: My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care Sdn Bhd is a licensed private elder care operator founded in 2008, running a network of small nursing home branches concentrated in Petaling Jaya. The company was established by medical professionals and describes its team as bringing over sixty years of combined healthcare experience. Rather than operating one large facility, the group has built a cluster of smaller properties across PJ — a model that keeps resident numbers low at each site while maintaining shared management, clinical protocols, and staffing oversight across the network.

The service scope covers residential nursing care, dementia care, bedridden and post-stroke care, post-operative recovery, palliative care, and respite admissions, with home nursing available through the same teams. The operating baseline across all branches is 24-hour nursing with hourly monitoring rounds and a doctor on-call at all times. Six meals a day are included, prepared to comply with JKM dietary standards. An in-house physiotherapy unit (MAC Physio Corner) supports stroke and surgical recovery. Room options range from dormitory to single occupancy in hotel-style settings. Network-wide pricing runs from RM 3,001 to RM 5,000 per month, with a base care package from RM 2,500 and physiotherapy available as an add-on.

Families considering My Aged Care benefit from the continuity of a single operator across multiple PJ branches — shared clinical records, protocols, and management make transfers between sites more practical than switching between unrelated operators. The small-unit model preserves the household atmosphere that large institutional wards tend to lose. Visit the specific branch you intend to use, confirm which services are available at that site rather than shared across locations, and ask about nurse-to-resident ratios overnight, dementia management protocols, and JKM licence status for that branch.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ @ My Manor
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care's My Manor is the flagship property of My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, a licensed elder care operator established in 2008. The home occupies a roughly 10,000-square-foot standalone building in PJS 7, Petaling Jaya, and is built around a deliberately low-density residential model — fewer residents per floor than a conventional multi-storey nursing home. The operator describes My Manor as its original branch and the anchor around which the broader My Aged Care network in PJ has expanded.

Care at My Manor follows the network's documented standard: 24-hour nursing with hourly monitoring rounds, a doctor on-call at all times, and six meals daily. The operator lists care categories that include nursing home care, dementia care, bedridden and post-stroke care, post-operative recovery, palliative care, and respite admissions. An in-house physiotherapy programme (MAC Physio Corner) supports residents in rehabilitation. Home nursing services are also available as an extension of the main offering. Room configurations range from dormitory sharing to single occupancy in hotel-style settings. Published network-wide pricing runs from RM 3,001 to RM 5,000 per month; physiotherapy is available as an add-on.

My Manor holds a 4.5-star rating across 142 Google reviews — a substantial base for a PJ nursing home of this scale. The low-density model is the defining characteristic: smaller resident numbers make personalised monitoring routines more viable and the atmosphere more domestic. Bed availability is consequently limited, so families are better served by enquiring early rather than under time pressure. Visit the property to see the layout firsthand, confirm nurse-to-resident ratios at night, ask how dementia behaviours are managed on-site, and verify JKM registration for this branch specifically.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ Old Town
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care's PJ Old Town branch occupies premises along Jalan Othman and Jalan Terus in the heart of Petaling Jaya's oldest commercial district, a short drive from Assunta Hospital and the broader PJ healthcare corridor. It is one of the residential branches operated by My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, a licensed elder care provider founded in 2008. The facility is registered as a nursing home and primarily serves elderly residents requiring full-time nursing support — including those stepping down from a hospital admission and those living with dementia or in need of palliative care.

The operating model is consistent across the My Aged Care network: 24-hour nursing with hourly monitoring rounds, a doctor on-call at all times, and six dietitian-approved meals daily. Laundry is included. Care categories documented on the operator's website span nursing home care, dementia, bedridden care, respite, post-operative and post-stroke rehabilitation, and palliative care. Room configurations range from dormitory to single occupancy in hotel-style settings. An in-house physiotherapy unit supports rehabilitation. Pricing across the network runs from RM 3,001 to RM 5,000 per month; a base care entry is listed from RM 2,500, with physiotherapy available as an add-on.

The PJ Old Town branch holds a 4.8-star rating across 92 Google reviews — the strongest score in the My Aged Care network, and a meaningful signal of sustained family satisfaction at this specific location. Its position in PJ Old Town provides practical road access for family visits, and Assunta Hospital is nearby for specialist follow-up. Because the group manages multiple branches under shared infrastructure, visit the specific property to confirm on-site staffing levels and which capabilities are housed at this location. Ask about nurse-to-resident ratios at night, the dementia unit setup, and JKM licence status for this branch.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in Petaling Jaya @ Jalan Assunta
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care's Jalan Assunta branch sits at 9 Jalan Assunta 4/52, Petaling Jaya, directly adjacent to Assunta Hospital — a location that is genuinely rare among PJ nursing homes and one of the most clinically practical positions an elder care facility can occupy in this part of Selangor. It is operated by My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, a licensed elder care provider founded in 2008, and is one of three residential branches the company runs across Petaling Jaya. The proximity to Assunta Hospital makes specialist review, acute transfers, and diagnostic appointments significantly more accessible than at more remote facilities.

The care scope matches the network's published standard: 24-hour nursing with hourly monitoring rounds, a doctor on-call at all times, and six meals daily. Care categories include nursing home care, bedridden and dementia care, respite, palliative care, and post-stroke and post-operative rehabilitation. Room configurations range from dormitory to single occupancy. Physiotherapy sessions are available as an add-on at RM 1,500 per month for twelve sessions. Network-wide pricing runs from RM 3,001 to RM 5,000 per month, with a base package from RM 2,500.

The branch holds a 4.4-star rating across 85 Google reviews. Its Google Maps category of "retirement home" understates the actual care scope — the operator's published services clearly include skilled nursing, dementia care, and palliative support. The defining practical advantage remains location: having Assunta Hospital as an immediate neighbour is particularly valuable for residents who require regular specialist follow-up or whose condition may need acute management at short notice. Visit the site to confirm current bed availability, ask about nurse-to-resident ratios overnight, and verify JKM registration for this specific branch.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home(Sungai Long & Kajang ElderCare Center) 加影碧绿疗养院
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen's Sungai Long and Kajang ElderCare Center is one of the founding locations of Mintygreen Healthcare Group, a Malaysian nursing home chain established in 2017. The branch operates from a six-shoplot block at Landmark Residences 2 in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang, and serves the southern Klang Valley catchment spanning Cheras, Balakong, Seri Kembangan, and Kajang town. Mintygreen positions itself as the first nursing home chain in Malaysia to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine into its care programme, and this runs across all branches including this one.

The facility accommodates residents from semi-dependent to fully bedridden, with long-term, short-term, and day care options. The group's documented clinical services include bi-weekly GP visits, NG tube and PEG feeding, stoma and catheter care, insulin administration, wound and bedsore management, and oxygen therapy. TCM practitioners provide acupuncture and Tui Na alongside conventional physiotherapy. The group holds AgeCope certification. Long-term care starts from RM 4,000 per month; day care is available at RM 220 per day. The branch is within 10–15 minutes of Columbia Asia Cheras, UKM Medical Centre, and KPJ Kajang Hospital — useful proximity for specialist follow-up and acute transfers.

The 4.6-star rating across 108 Google reviews reflects a well-established presence for a chain founded in 2017. The TCM integration distinguishes Mintygreen from most Malaysian nursing homes and tends to resonate with Chinese-speaking families who value complementary therapy alongside conventional nursing. The shoplot format does constrain outdoor space; ask about communal activity areas during a site visit. Confirm current bed availability, JKM registration for this specific location, and whether TCM sessions are bundled into the monthly fee or charged separately.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Senior Care Center Cheras(Sungai Long) 蕉赖碧绿疗养院
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Senior Care Center, marked on Google Maps as Cheras (Sungai Long), is a branch of Mintygreen Healthcare Group that serves the Cheras-to-Kajang corridor. The facility is at Landmark Residences 2, Jalan Sungai Long, Kajang — the Sungai Long site that anchors the group's presence in the southern Klang Valley. Mintygreen was founded in 2017 and distinguishes itself within the Malaysian market as an early integrator of Traditional Chinese Medicine into its nursing home model, a feature present across all its branches.

Care spans from semi-dependent to fully bedridden residents, with day care and short-term respite available alongside long-term residency. Clinical services documented across the Mintygreen network include bi-weekly visiting GP consultations, NG tube feeding, stoma and catheter care, insulin administration, pressure ulcer prevention and management, and oxygen therapy. TCM practitioners deliver acupuncture and Tui Na alongside conventional physiotherapy. The group holds AgeCope certification. Long-term care is priced from RM 4,000 per month; day care at RM 220 per day. The branch is within 10–15 minutes of Columbia Asia Cheras, UKM Medical Centre, Kajang Hospital, and KPJ Kajang.

Bed count and pricing beyond the network's starting rate are not listed publicly for this specific listing; enquire directly for current availability and a quotation. The bi-weekly GP visit cadence is standard across the group — for residents with complex or acute medical needs, ask how between-visit deteriorations are escalated and whether specialist review can be arranged. The TCM element is the clearest differentiator from a conventional nursing home; confirm which therapies are bundled in the base fee versus charged additionally before making a commitment.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing & Retirement Home (Kepong and PJ Bandar Sri Damansara)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen's Bandar Sri Damansara branch occupies a commercial block at Sphere Damansara, Jalan PJU 10/9 in Prima Damansara — a location at the KL-Selayang border that draws families from Kepong, Sri Damansara, Damansara Perdana, and the northern PJ corridor. It is part of Mintygreen Healthcare Group, founded in 2017 and known within the Malaysian eldercare market as the first nursing home chain to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine into its care model. The group holds AgeCope certification and operates on a bilingual Chinese-English basis across its network.

The branch serves residents from semi-ambulant to fully bedridden, with day care and short-term respite alongside long-term placements. Documented clinical services across the Mintygreen network include NG tube and PEG feeding, catheter care, insulin management, and pressure ulcer prevention and management. TCM physicians provide acupuncture and Tui Na on-site. A visiting GP attends bi-weekly. Post-stroke, post-cancer, and post-operative recovery tracks are listed as supported care types. Long-term care starts from RM 4,000 per month; day care at RM 220 per day.

The 4.1-star rating across 50 Google reviews sits slightly below the Mintygreen network average, so a direct visit to assess the physical environment and staffing at this specific branch is particularly worthwhile. The Sphere Damansara commercial block setting differs from a bungalow or shoplot format — ask about common areas, outdoor access, and how ambulant residents spend their day. Bed capacity for this branch is not publicly disclosed; pricing beyond the published starting rate requires a direct enquiry. Confirm JKM registration for this location and what is included in the monthly fee versus charged separately.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Suites - Home Care Nursing Cheras KL
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Suites operates at 100, Jalan Cheras, Phoenix Business Park in Kuala Lumpur, a short walk from the Taman Connaught MRT station and on the Cheras-Kajang corridor. Unlike Mintygreen's shoplot and bungalow-format branches, this outlet is purpose-built around hotel-suite rooms: private, en-suite spaces with lift access and commercial-grade fire safety infrastructure. It is operated as a distinct entity within the Mintygreen Healthcare Group and marketed as a boutique care setting for residents who want the nursing capability of a nursing home with significantly more room privacy.

Documented services include 24-hour nursing care, day care and overnight respite, hospice and palliative care, NG tube feeding, urinary catheter care, wound and bedsore management, post-operative recovery, medication administration, and fortnightly doctor visits. Physiotherapy is available across the Mintygreen network; TCM services — acupuncture and Tui Na — are part of the group's standard offering. The nearest acute hospitals are Pantai Hospital Cheras (approximately 3.3 km), Sunway Velocity Medical Centre (approximately 6.6 km), and Hospital Canselor Tuanku Muhriz (approximately 5 km). Long-term care across the Mintygreen network starts from RM 4,000 per month; this branch's hotel-suite format may carry different pricing and should be confirmed directly.

The 4.8-star rating across 21 reviews is the strongest in the Mintygreen network. The suite-format model is the clearest differentiator: families who prioritise private en-suite rooms over shared accommodation — particularly for short-term recovery stays — will find it worth considering. Bed count and exact room rates for this specific outlet are not publicly listed. Confirm what is included in the monthly fee, whether the kitchen is halal-certified, nurse staffing overnight, and the JKM Act 506 licence number for the Cheras outlet specifically.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21, Subang Jaya
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Centre at USJ21 is a neighbourhood-scale branch of Mintygreen Healthcare Group, operating from 1, Jalan USJ 21/10 in UEP Subang Jaya. Mintygreen was founded in 2017 and has expanded across the Klang Valley on a model that integrates Traditional Chinese Medicine alongside conventional nursing care — a combination that remains uncommon in Malaysia's nursing home sector. The USJ21 branch brings this to a mature Subang Jaya township, within reasonable distance of Subang Jaya Medical Centre and several clinics in the area.

Across the Mintygreen network, documented capabilities include 24-hour nursing care, post-stroke and post-operative recovery, bi-weekly doctor visits, physiotherapy, TCM services (acupuncture and Tui Na), dietetic support, day care, and home care through the group's 24 Angels arm. The USJ21 branch is a smaller, neighbourhood-scale operation relative to the group's larger Sungai Long and Kajang sites, and not all network capabilities can be assumed present at every location — confirm which are on-site. The group holds AgeCope certification. Long-term care across the network starts from RM 4,000 per month; day care at RM 220 per day.

For families in Subang Jaya or USJ who want a local option without travelling to Kajang or central PJ, this branch is a practical starting point. The smaller scale may translate to a more personal atmosphere, though it also means limited total capacity. A site visit is the most useful next step: it will reveal the physical space, current bed availability, and what share of the network's full services are actually on-site. Ask about doctor visit frequency, how complex residents are managed between bi-weekly GP visits, TCM session pricing, and JKM registration for this branch.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home (ElderCare Center)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home (ElderCare Center) is the Sungai Long headquarters of Mintygreen Healthcare Group, a Malaysian eldercare chain founded in 2017. The facility operates from a six-shoplot block at Landmark Residences 2 in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang — the group's anchor branch and its longest-running site. It holds a 4.6-star rating across over a hundred Google reviews, making it the most review-tested branch in the Mintygreen network. The group markets itself as the first nursing home chain in Malaysia to integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine as a core element of its care programme.

The Sungai Long branch handles the full residential range from semi-dependent residents to those who are fully bedridden. The documented service list includes day and long-term care, hospice and palliative care, wound management, NG tube and catheter care, medication administration, and bi-weekly GP visits. TCM services — acupuncture, Tui Na, and TCM physician consultations — are offered alongside conventional physiotherapy. The group holds AgeCope certification. Rooms have call bells, wheelchair-accessible bathrooms, and grab bars throughout. Staff at Mintygreen include nurses, physiotherapists, TCM physicians, mental health practitioners, and counsellors. Long-term care starts from RM 4,000 per month; day care at RM 220 per day.

This is a mid-to-upper market option, particularly suited to Chinese-speaking families who value TCM alongside conventional nursing. The 100-plus Google reviews give families more signal to evaluate than most nursing homes of this size. Before committing, ask about current bed availability at the Sungai Long site, nurse-to-resident ratios overnight, JKM licence status for this branch, dementia management protocols, and which services are bundled in the monthly fee versus charged per session.

facilities.title: Noble Care Malaysia
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care Malaysia is one of the country's larger managed eldercare networks, operating more than sixteen nursing homes across West Malaysia alongside a home nursing arm. The group has been active since 2005 and runs residential care branches across the Klang Valley and beyond under the Noble Care brand. This listing represents the Noble Care home nursing service rather than a physical residential facility — for families who need professional care delivered at home rather than a residential bed, the service sends trained nurses and caregivers to the client's residence.

Documented capabilities in the home nursing service include post-surgical wound care, medication management, chronic disease monitoring, IV therapy, Foley's catheter care, nasogastric tube management, tracheostomy care, colostomy bag changes, bedsore dressing, physiotherapy, and palliative support. This is a clinically substantive range that extends well into complex medical territory, and it positions Noble Care's home nursing as a meaningful option for residents discharged from hospital who require skilled nursing at home. Arrangements are available full-time, part-time, or on an hourly basis, giving families flexibility to build a package around existing family capacity.

Pricing is quoted after an initial consultation rather than published online — standard practice in Malaysia's home care sector where needs vary considerably. At consultation, ask about the qualifications and registration status of nurses assigned to complex cases such as tracheostomy or PEG tube management, the process for replacing a carer who is absent or not a suitable fit, and how medical deterioration is escalated when the carer is on-site. Noble Care's scale as a national operator provides operational structure that smaller agencies may lack, but confirm which branch or team will be managing the specific household to ensure clear local accountability.

facilities.title: Noble Care Nursing Home | Nursing Home Subang Jaya Selangor
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care at USJ 18 is one branch within Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd., a national eldercare operator running more than sixteen locations across Malaysia. The facility is at 1, Jalan USJ 18/3 in Subang Jaya — a mature township with good road access via the Federal Highway and LDP. Operating under a shared corporate framework means Noble Care USJ 18 follows the same standardised care model and oversight structure as other branches in the network, which provides institutional accountability that a purely family-run home may not have.

The operator's website lists care categories for this branch that include general senior residential care, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and cancer care, stroke care, respite stays, and post-operative recovery. Clinical capabilities include catheter management, nasogastric tube feeding, colostomy care, tracheostomy care, and wound dressing — a range suggesting capacity for medically complex residents beyond basic supervised living. Physiotherapy is also listed. The facility describes 24-hour medical supervision as standard.

Bed count and pricing are not published on the website; a consultation is needed to assess costs and current availability. The 3.8-star rating across 21 Google reviews is worth reviewing in detail — read the specific feedback text and ask the manager how issues raised have been addressed since those reviews were posted. On a visit, confirm that the USJ 18 branch has its own permanently assigned registered nurse on overnight duty rather than staff rotating across branches, and verify the JKM licence number for this specific address. Because Noble Care runs multiple sites, also clarify which clinical capabilities are available at this branch versus centralised at other locations.
